name: ETH Session Drive Continuation v1
thesis: ETH 4h is the best-fit primary lane for a session-drive continuation idea only if it is rewritten as dense post-impulse hold logic rather than a literal opening-range ritual. The edge should come from persistence after a meaningful directional bar that holds value, not from exact session-clock choreography.
invalidation_condition: Abort if post-impulse hold entries remain too sparse, if impulse bars mostly mean-revert instead of persisting, or if the continuation leg is too weak after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A meaningful directional expansion bar shifts short-term order flow, and continuation becomes tradable only if the next bar holds above reclaimed value rather than fully retracing.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Even in chop, some expansions create brief persistence. Trading only after the hold should be denser than exact session-window logic while still respecting the original follow-through hypothesis.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the cleanest lane for testing this because the bars are large enough to show real expansion and hold structure without relying on noisy intrabar timing.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market stays fully rotational, impulse bars may revert too quickly for a hold-based continuation to persist.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but the runner underperforms, keep the entry and shorten the back-half exit ambition.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; impulse, hold, and participation are complementary
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by using broad impulse-and-hold logic instead of exact session windows or breakout sequences

long: Close - Open >= 0.35 * ATR_14 OR Close[1] - Open[1] >= 0.35 * ATR_14
short: Open - Close >= 0.35 * ATR_14 OR Open[1] - Close[1] >= 0.35 * ATR_14
